Title: Police open probe into delayed burial at Hulu Kelang Muslim cemetery
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Police have opened a criminal investigation into the alleged forgery of a signature linked to the delayed burial of a deceased person at the Ukay Perdana Muslim Cemetery in Hulu Kelang.
Commercial Crime Investigation Department (CCID) director Datuk Rusdi Mohd Isa said an investigation paper had been opened under Section 468 of the Penal Code for forgery for the purpose of cheating.
"An investigation paper has been opened under Section 468 of the Penal Code in relation to the alleged forgery of a signature for the purpose of burying a deceased person," he said in a brief statement today.
The latest development comes after Ampang Jaya police confirmed they had received a report from the
deceased's family
over delays in the burial, which recently went viral on social media.
Ampang Jaya police chief Assistant Commissioner Khairul Anuar Khalid said the report was lodged by the deceased's next of kin and investigations were ongoing.
The issue surfaced after the Selangor Islamic Religious Department (Jais) denied allegations circulating on social media that the delay was caused by the mosque handling the burial arrangements.
Jais director Mohd Shahzihan Ahmad said investigations found the delay stemmed from confusion after the family initially entrusted the burial arrangements to Badan Kebajikan Salatulrahim (BKS), which has no official link to the mosque.
He also dismissed claims that the mosque had demanded RM1,400 before allowing the burial to proceed.
According to Jais, the deceased died at 6.22am on June 25 and the required burial application form was issued by Masjid Nurul Hidayah after the original death registration document was submitted.
However, the mosque was later informed that the deceased's grandfather wanted BKS to continue managing the burial arrangements.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China mulls revisions to gold import, export regulations
Article:
BEIJING: Chinese regulators have proposed revisions to current regulations on the import and export of gold and gold products, aiming to streamline administration, facilitate trade, and improve the management of gold carried across the border by individuals, reported Xinhua.
The People's Bank of China (PBOC) said the draft amendments were jointly formulated with the General Administration of Customs to update the existing regulatory framework in line with evolving economic conditions, legal requirements and policy adjustments.
Under the draft rules, the provision requiring the PBOC and customs authorities to jointly formulate rules for individuals carrying or mailing gold and gold products across the border would be removed.
To further optimise management, the draft also states that such cross-border movements will remain subject to customs supervision.
The revisions also seek to improve convenience for businesses and the public by formalising measures that have proven effective in practice.
In addition, the draft would strengthen ex-ante supervision by clarifying the scope of customs oversight, enhancing supervision of foreign trade companies acting as agents, and improving the penalty framework for violations, according to the central bank.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 'Why did you leave so soon?' Widow mourns husband killed in expressway crash
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The widow of a motorcyclist who was killed after plunging about 30m from the Setiawangsa-Pantai Expressway (SPE) has shared an emotional tribute to her late husband, saying she is struggling to come to terms with raising their children alone.
In a post on Threads, the woman, known only as Nurbaizura, shared what she described as the last photograph of their family together and expressed her grief four days after her husband's death.
"My love, today is the fourth day since you left me and our children. How am I supposed to live and raise our children without you?
"Why did you leave so soon? It happened so suddenly. I didn't have enough time to be the wife you deserved. I didn't have enough time to make you truly happy. Please forgive me.
"Al-Fatihah to my husband, my soulmate, Muhammad Shafeeq Hashim," she wrote.
Another Threads user, identified as Shahrulkamal and believed to be a close friend of the deceased, described Muhammad Shafeeq as a devoted husband and father who worked tirelessly to support his family.
"He was a very good husband and father. He worked multiple jobs to provide for his family.
"He had only secured a permanent position with my brother's company earlier this week, but fate had other plans and Allah called him home because of a careless driver.
"I'm sorry. I never got the chance to offer you the job we talked about. We'll meet again one day. Thank you for being such a good person," he wrote.
Muhammad Shafeeq, 31, died in the accident at KM15.8 of the SPE towards Sungai Besi on Thursday.
https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2026/06/1472889/man-dies-after-being-flung-30-metres-spe-bridge-multi-vehicle-crash
Police said he was believed to have been sheltering from heavy rain beneath a flyover when a Proton X50, driven by a 21-year-old woman, allegedly lost control, veered onto the road shoulder and struck two motorcycles.
The impact threw Muhammad Shafeeq from the flyover.
Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said the crash involved the Proton X50 and two motorcycles.
He said the driver of the sport utility vehicle sustained minor injuries, while a second motorcyclist suffered a fractured leg.
The case is being investigated under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987 for causing death by reckless or dangerous driving.
Police have urged witnesses or anyone with information about the incident to contact the Kuala Lumpur Traffic Investigation and Enforcement Department or the nearest police station to assist investigations.
The case remains under investigation.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Myvi in 3-vehicle crash had broken down on emergency lane before hit by lorry
Article:
SUNGAI PETANI: Police confirmed that a Perodua Myvi involved in an accident this morning had stopped on the emergency lane due to a breakdown, before it was rear-ended by a lorry.
Kuala Muda district police chief Assistant Commissioner Hanyan Ramlan said the
9am accident
involved the Perodua Myvi, a Toyota Noah, and a lorry at Km94 of the North-South Expressway (southbound) near here.
"Initial investigations revealed that the Toyota Noah had stopped in front of the stalled Perodua Myvi. A lorry coming from behind then crashed into the back of the Myvi.
"The impact caused the Myvi to ram into the back of the Toyota Noah. All three vehicles veered off to the shoulder of the highway as a result of the collision," he said in a statement.
Perodua Myvi driver Noriah Mat Isa, 56, who is the senior assistant for the Integrated Special Education Programme at SK Utan Aji in Perlis, suffered severe injuries.
She is receiving treatment in the red zone of Sultan Abdul Halim Hospital here.
Hanyan said only one person was severely injured, while the other seven sustained minor injuries. The case is being investigated under Section 43 of the Road Transport Act 1987.
Noriah, along with five other teachers and her daughter, Nur Aalia Jeffri, 16, were on their way to SK Dato Haji Zainuddin in Serdang, Kedah, to attend a programme after leaving early in the morning.
Meanwhile, Guar Chempedak Fire and Rescue station chief Assistant Fire Superintendent Mohd Happyzam Abd Rashid said they received an emergency call at 8.56am.
Firefighters used rescue equipment to extricate Noriah, who was trapped in the driver's seat, before handing her over to Health Ministry personnel for further medical attention, he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Migrants pay up to RM6,000 each to be smuggled into Malaysia by sea, says MMEA
Article:
PASIR PUTEH: Migrants are believed to be paying agents up to RM6,000 each to be smuggled by sea into Malaysia for work, the Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) said.
The revelation came after the arrest of eight Myanmar nationals in separate operations last Wednesday.
Kelantan MMEA director Kamal Mohamad said the migrants comprised five men and three women, aged between 18 and 51, who claimed to have paid RM6,000 each for a four-day sea journey to secure factory jobs.
"At 11.47pm, we deployed our assets to Sungai Kelantan and spotted a poorly lit boat speeding away.
"At 12.20am, however, the boat approached the riverbank and dropped off passengers. We then moved in and arrested eight undocumented migrants," he said at a press conference at the Kelantan MMEA headquarters in Tok Bali today.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Chery boosts presence in Malaysia
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Chinese automaker Chery says it has expanded its presence in Malaysia to 60 dealerships nationwide and launched its 10th vehicle model, three years after re-entering the market.
The Tiggo 9 marks the company's 10th vehicle introduced in Malaysia and broadens its SUV range.
A key milestone in Chery's expansion is the development of the RM2.2 billion Chery Smart Auto Industrial Park in Lembah Beringin, Selangor.
The 80.94ha facility, which recently completed the topping-out of its first phase, is set to become the company's largest assembly plant in Malaysia.
"The automotive industry thrives on competition, and we welcome fair competition," Chery said.
"Ultimately, the strongest endorsement of any brand comes from its customers whose confidence in its products will continue to be the backbone of its growth in Malaysia."
Beyond its commercial expansion, Chery said it has continued to invest in customer engagement through its With Chery With Love community platform, which organises lifestyle, family and community activities for owners.
The company has also stepped up its environmental and community initiatives.
Earlier this year, it launched "Cherish the Nature", an 18-month mangrove restoration programme along the North-Central Selangor Coast in collaboration with the International Union for Conservation of Nature and the Malaysian Nature Society.
Chery also provided 50 vehicles to residents affected by the Putra Heights gas pipe explosion in Selangor last year.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: Pas says no cooperation with Bersatu despite PN ties
Article:
KOTA BARU: Pas has denied claims that it is backing Bersatu in the Johor election, insisting the party is merely honouring seat allocations within Perikatan Nasional (PN) despite the absence of political cooperation on the ground.
Pas vice-president Datuk Mohd Amar Nik Abdullah said there was no question of the party supporting Bersatu, as Bersatu remained a component party of PN.
He was responding to Amanah vice-president Datuk Mahfuz Omar, who had questioned Pas over its decision to allow Bersatu to contest several seats in the state election.
"Why should we be accused of backing Bersatu? Bersatu is still a member of PN.
"Why is he (Mahfuz) so busy with our affairs? He should take care of his own party," said the Panchor assemblyman.
Amar said the seats allocated to Bersatu were those requested by the party itself, based on constituencies it believed it had the potential to win.
"We gave Bersatu those seats because it is still part of PN. Those were the seats they requested and identified as having winning potential.
"But there is no political cooperation between Pas and Bersatu in the Johor election," he said.
Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang was reported as saying that the two parties would use
separate machinery
and campaign strategies, although they would share the PN logo in the July 11 polls.
Mahfuz had
questioned Pas
over its continued backing of Bersatu candidates in the Johor election despite serious allegations made by the party against its Perikatan Nasional ally.
Mahfuz said Pas leaders had previously accused Bersatu of betrayal in Perlis, undermining political efforts in Negri Sembilan, violating syariah principles in political cooperation, weakening unity within PN, being greedy in seat negotiations, lacking grassroots machinery and taking advantage of Pas.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Onn Hafiz: Pas' call to back BN part of party's strategy
Article:
SIMPANG RENGGAM: All political parties are free to determine their own election strategies, including Pas' call for its members to support BN candidates in constituencies not contested by Perikatan Nasional (PN).
Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, who is also the caretaker menteri besar, said the matter was entirely up to the respective parties.
"We are free to plan our own strategies.
"Whatever Pas does, whatever PN does, that is up to their respective strategies," he said when met at the Simpang Renggam Prisons Family Day and a casual engagement programme here today.
Onn Hafiz was responding to reports that Pas had instructed its members and supporters to vote for Umno or BN candidates in state seats where PN is not contesting in the Johor election.
Pas deputy president Datuk Seri Tuan Ibrahim Tuan Man was quoted as saying urging Pas members and supporters not to vote for PH in constituencies where PN is absent from the contest. ((hyperlink: https://www.nst.com.my/news/regional/2026/06/1474855/tuan-ibrahim-avoid-split-votes-back-umno-non-pn-seats)
The Johor election will see contests in all 56 state constituencies. PH is contesting all seats, fielding candidates from PKR, Amanah and DAP, while BN is also contesting all constituencies.
Yesterday, BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi did not rule out the possibility of internal discussions on cooperation involving Pas and Parti Wawasan Negara (Wawasan) in the Johor election. (hyperlink:
https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2026/06/1474127/zahid-does-not-rule-out-lower-level-leader-talks-between-bn-pas-and)
However, he said such discussions, if they had taken place, were likely held at lower levels within the party.
"In politics, there are always discussions on various possibilities. But if those possibilities do not materialise, then we stick to contesting solo."
Umno Supreme Council Member Datuk Lokman Noor Adam previously confirmed that there would be renewed cooperation with Pas in the state election, despite earlier saying he did not want to be "bitten by the same snake twice".
He said it was a second chance for Umno to re-establish cooperation with Pas, with the possibility of including Wawasan.
On June 8, Tuan Ibrahim was also reported as saying that talks on cooperation between the Islamist party and Umno — facilitated through an internal committee involving both parties — had yet to produce any positive outcome.
He said claims that Pas had been offered exco posts and other appointments by Umno, particularly ahead of the Johor, Negri Sembilan and Melaka elections, were merely rumours.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Budi Madani portal receives 13,000 new applications as of 11am: Finance Ministry
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The official Budi Madani portal has received over 13,000 new applications as of 11 am today for an additional 100 litres of subsidised diesel per month for eligible private pick-up truck and jeep owners.
The Ministry of Finance (MOF), in a statement today, said this addition increases the total diesel subsidy quota to 300 litres per month for eligible vehicle owners.
"The first day of the early access rollout for the Budi Madani Diesel (BUDI Diesel) initiative for eligible private diesel vehicle owners in Peninsular Malaysia on June 27 ran smoothly.
The MyKad verification system at petrol stations worked well, with no serious complaints or issues reported.
"Throughout the first day of early access on June 27, nearly 24,000 transactions were recorded, involving the purchase of almost one million litres of subsidised diesel worth RM2.1 million at RM2.15 per litre," the statement read.
MOF added that the early access rollout prepares for the full nationwide implementation of Budi Diesel starting July 1, which will enable eligible citizens to purchase subsidised diesel at RM2.10 per litre via MyKad verification.
According to the ministry, diesel subsidy rationalisation is implemented to ensure the country's subsidised diesel supply remains sufficient for citizens and sectors that genuinely need it. It also aims to curb leakages and ensure targeted delivery.
"For the public transport, logistics and eligible commercial vehicle sectors, the diesel subsidy will continue to be channelled through the Subsidised Diesel Control System (SKDS) under the Ministry of Domestic Trade and Cost of Living, according to their respective categories and operational needs," the MOF statement added.
It said the government will continue to monitor the Budi Diesel early access phase and fine-tune its execution based on feedback from the public and industry.
"The public can check their eligibility status, apply for the additional Budi Diesel quota or obtain further information through the official Budi Madani portal, while SKDS applications can be made through KPDN's MySubsidi portal," said the MOF.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Golok 'fun' trip ends in arrest for two men over illegal entry at Tumpat jetty
Article:
TUMPAT: Two men were detained by the General Operations Force (GOF) after they were caught entering the country from Thailand via an illegal jetty in Kampung Mentua here yesterday evening.
The suspects, 33 and 45, are believed to have travelled to Thailand to visit an entertainment outlet in Golok before returning to Malaysia through an unauthorised route.
GOF Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said his team spotted the two men on a boat approaching the riverbank while conducting surveillance at the location.
"Both men were detained. Urine tests conducted on the suspects also returned positive for drugs.
"Both admitted boarding the boat from Thailand and paying RM15 each to an unidentified boat operator.
"They also claimed they had travelled to Thailand at 2pm the previous day to visit an entertainment outlet in Golok," he said in a statement today.
He said the suspects were handed over to the Tumpat district police headquarters.
The case is being investigated under Section 5(2) of the Immigration Act and Section 15(1) of the Dangerous Drugs Act.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bank Muamalat unveils initiatives to boost women participation in Islamic finance
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bank Muamalat Malaysia Bhd has launched two new initiatives aimed at strengthening Islamic financial inclusion, introducing a women-focused takaful solution and a digital inheritance planning tool in conjunction with the Karnival Celik Kewangan Terengganu 2026.
The initiatives comprise M Zahra Takaful MyLadies, a dedicated protection plan for women, and a Digital Faraid Calculator developed in collaboration with As-Salihin Trustee Bhd.
Bank Muamalat said the launches reflect its ongoing commitment to expanding access to Islamic financial solutions that are more inclusive, practical and aligned with current societal needs, particularly in areas such as financial protection, family wellbeing and legacy planning.
M Zahra Takaful MyLadies is designed to support women through different stages of life by providing financial protection, medical benefits, savings elements and legacy planning features aligned with modern lifestyles.
The Syariah-compliant solution offers five tailored packages namely Safiya, Amara, Rifaah, Namaa and Maryam, with monthly contributions from RM50.
Bank Muamalat also extends contributions to four single mother associations in Terengganu, reflecting its support for women empowerment and wellbeing.
Meanwhile, the Digital Faraid Calculator is designed to help Muslims estimate inheritance distribution in a simple, fast and accurate way in accordance with Faraid principles and Islamic law.
The platform aims to improve public understanding of estate management and encourage earlier, more structured legacy planning.
The bank noted that the initiative comes at a timely moment as issues surrounding unclaimed estates and inheritance disputes continue to draw public attention, highlighting the importance of greater awareness in wealth management.
Bank Muamalat president and chief executive officer Datuk Khairul Kamarudin said women today play an increasingly important role not only within families but also as economic contributors and financial decision makers.
"M-Zahra Takaful MyLadies was developed not merely as a protection product, but as a comprehensive solution that understands the life journey and needs of today's women," he said in a statement.
He said the Digital Faraid Calculator reflects Bank Muamalat's commitment to strengthening Islamic financial literacy through the provision of Syariah-compliant digital solutions that are more accessible and practical.
"Proper estate planning not only helps ensure that asset distribution is carried out fairly in accordance with Syariah, but also plays an important role in preserving family harmony and providing peace of mind for future heirs," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: BN's mix of experience, fresh faces key to governing - Khaled
Article:
KLUANG: Barisan Nasional's (BN) blend of experienced leaders and fresh faces for the 16th Johor state election is the best formula for meeting the increasingly complex challenges of governing, Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said.
He said criticism that BN was relying heavily on 'recycled' candidates should be viewed in the context of the need to ensure continuity in leadership and experience within the state administration.
He said fielding an entirely new line-up would amount to an unnecessary gamble at a time when experienced leaders are needed to navigate increasingly complex challenges.
"We must remember that the situation we face today is very complex. If we field only new candidates, it would be like experimenting. We simply cannot afford that.
"That is why the best approach is to have a combination of experienced and new candidates," he told reporters after attending the Southern Zone Armed Forces Family Day Carnival at X Park Impiana 2 Square, Bandar Seri Impian, here today.
The defence minister and Kota Tinggi MP said experience is crucial to ensuring a government can function effectively once entrusted with the people's mandate.
He said BN was not fielding new faces merely to satisfy public perception, but was instead prioritising candidates capable of delivering on their responsibilities if elected.
"We are not trying to showcase an entirely new line-up only for them to be unsure where to begin after winning. What matters is the ability to work and serve the people," he said.
He added that Johoreans need a strong and stable government to address current challenges and continue the state's development agenda, stressing that elections should enable voters to choose a government capable of delivering a better future.
Khaled also argued that only BN and Pakatan Harapan (PH) are in a position to form the next Johor state government based on the number of seats they are contesting.
Following nominations yesterday, BN and PH are each contesting all 56 seats, while Perikatan Nasional (PN), through Pas and Bersatu, is contesting 27 seats.
"If you look at the nominations, only BN and PH have the numbers to form the state government. Pas is contesting 11 seats and Bersatu 16. Even combined, they cannot form the state government," he said.
Meanwhile, in
PONTIAN
, Johor BN deputy chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan said the selection of all 56 candidates was based on a thorough assessment, taking into account a balanced mix of new faces, experienced leaders, women and youth candidates.
The Umno supreme council member said BN had also fielded technocrats and professionals from various backgrounds, including lawyers, doctors and engineers.
"We are highly confident because the candidates presented to the people have been assessed from every angle," he told a press conference after officiating the Meh Lah Pontian Community Programme: Kelana Keringat in conjunction with the National International Museum Day Festival 2026.
Yesterday, former Umno Supreme Council member Datuk Dr
Puad Zarkashi
expressed his disappointment over the party's candidate line-up, which he described as "recycled and mediocre".
Polling is on July 11, with early voting on July 7.
For the latest updates on the 16th Johor state election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/johor/.
– Bernama
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Jakim seeks tighter vetting of Quranic, Hadith content in books
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: The Malaysian Islamic Development Department (Jakim) has urged the National Book Council of Malaysia to remind publishers to exercise greater care when publishing religious books containing Quranic and Hadith texts.
Director‑general Datuk Dr Sirajuddin Suhaimee said in a statement today that the department, together with state Islamic religious authorities, was ready to provide guidance to publishers of religious books to ensure publications comply with syariah requirements.
The remarks followed controversy over errors in a religious book written by a social media influencer.
He said the department supported the firm stance taken by Minister in the Prime Minister's Department (Religious Affairs) Dr Zulkifli Hasan, who had urged publishers to preserve the sanctity of Islam and ensure religious books are reviewed by the relevant authorities.
Sirajuddin said authors must take full responsibility for the books they produce, while publishers of religious books should appoint a team of experts and personnel qualified in Islamic studies to review all religious content before publication. — BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Public hospitals can't bear healthcare burden alone, says deputy minister
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Public hospitals cannot shoulder the country's healthcare needs alone, says Deputy Health Minister Datuk Hanifah Hajar Taib.
She said while hospitals remain the backbone of the healthcare system, a strong primary care network, including private clinics, is crucial to achieving better health outcomes and long-term sustainability.
"Malaysia's healthcare system must become more sustainable and responsive to the changing needs of the population," she said at a memorandum of understanding (MoU) signing between the Malaysian Medical Association, IHH Healthcare Malaysia and Monash University Malaysia.
The MoU aims to strengthen healthcare delivery through better coordination, professional development, research and patient-centred care.
Hanifah said the initiative aligns with the national agenda to strengthen primary healthcare services and ensure the system remains responsive to the evolving needs of the people.
She added that progress in healthcare should not be measured solely by technology, equipment or treatment outcomes, but also by how well patients understand their conditions and receive support throughout their journey.
"General practitioners play a vital role because they are often the first point of contact when patients have concerns about their health.
"Consultation can help detect health problems early, guide patients towards appropriate treatment and prevent conditions from becoming more serious," she said.
She added that this is increasingly important as Malaysia faces a rising burden of non-communicable diseases such as diabetes, hypertension, obesity, heart disease and cancer.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: PH flag burned in Tanjung Surat, report lodged
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: Pakatan Harapan (PH) Tanjung Surat candidate Faizul Abdul Ghani has condemned what he described as the first alleged act of sabotage in the Johor election campaign, after a party flag in Felda Gugusan Adela was set on fire.
He said sabotage should not have a place in a mature democracy.
He said the incident was discovered at 2am today when a member of the PH logistics team found the flag burnt along a road leading into the Felda settlement.
A report was lodged with the Election Commission (EC) and the police, he said in a statement.
He said political differences must never be translated into acts of provocation or vandalism, stressing that such behaviour erodes democratic values.
"Differences in political ideology should not be expressed through provocation, sabotage or actions that undermine a mature democratic culture," he said.
He said PH respects all parties' right to campaign and express their views, but stressed that political freedom must be exercised responsibly and within the law.
The Tanjung Surat state seat is among 14 constituencies in Johor witnessing straight fights in the state election.
Barisan Nasional's Aznan will face PKR's Faizul.
Faizul also urged supporters and campaign workers to remain calm and not respond in ways that could breach legal boundaries, saying elections should be a platform for ideas and policy competition rather than destructive acts.
"The campaign should focus on offering ideas, track records and a better future for the people, not be marred by petty acts of sabotage," he said.
Meanwhile, district police chief Superintendent Yusof Othman said a police report was lodged at 9.50am today.
The case is being investigated under Section 435 of the Penal Code for mischief by fire or explosive substance.
He urged all parties to abide by Election Commission regulations and conduct their campaigns in a lawful and orderly manner, reminding them that any form of provocation or vandalism will not be tolerated.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: DNA results of DUKE crash victims expected tomorrow
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: DNA test results to confirm the identities of the two university students killed in a fiery crash on the Duta-Ulu Kelang Expressway (DUKE) are expected to be ready tomorrow.
Kuala Lumpur Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department chief Assistant Commissioner Mohd Zamzuri Mohd Isa said investigators were awaiting the outcome of the deoxyribonucleic acid (DNA) analysis.
"The DNA results are expected tomorrow," he said when contacted today.
Mohd Zamzuri added that police had also recorded the statement of the 20-year-old Singaporean passenger who survived the crash.
He said the burnt BMW 328i Sport involved in the incident would be sent to the Computerised Vehicle Inspection Centre (Puspakom) for further examination to assist investigations.
Police had earlier said they believe the
BMW had been speeding
before the crash.
The two victims were the 22-year-old driver and his 21-year-old passenger, both university students.
The driver, who was studying at an Australian university, lost control of the car before crashing into a guardrail.
The impact caused the vehicle to burst into flames. The third occupant, a Singaporean, managed to escape before the BMW was engulfed in flames, while the two victims were trapped inside.
The survivor suffered minor injuries to his hands and legs and was taken to Kuala Lumpur Hospital for treatment.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 6 students held for bullying after 14-year-old quits school in Muar
Article:
MUAR: Police have arrested six Form Five students over the bullying and extortion of a 14-year-old hostel student who was left traumatised and quit school.
The Form Two student lodged a police report at 5pm yesterday, alleging he was assaulted by senior students at a school here in May.
Police arrested the six 17-year-old suspects in a series of raids in Muar and Alor Gajah, Melaka, between 1.30am and 2.12am today.
The suspects, who have no prior criminal record, have been remanded for two days until tomorrow to assist investigations.
The victim claimed he was punched in the chest and headbutted in the face, leaving him with bruising around his eyes, and was forced to hand over money to the suspects.
Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the victim also claimed to have suffered emotional distress and trauma to the extent that he stopped attending school.
The suspects are being investigated under Section 507B of the Penal Code for causing harassment, distress, fear or anxiety, and Section 323 of the Penal Code for voluntarily causing hurt.
Police are also investigating possible negligence by the school hostel wardens and management under Section 31(1)(a) of the Child Act, as the alleged bullying took place at the hostel.
The offence is punishable by up to 20 years' imprisonment, a fine of up to RM50,000, or both.
Ab Rahaman urged other students who may have been bullied by the same suspects to come forward and lodge police reports.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Affordable housing, retail spaces planned along Shah Alam LRT3 line
Article:
SHAH ALAM: The government is looking into developing land owned by Prasarana around the Shah Alam LRT3 line stations under a Transit-Oriented Development (TOD) plan, says Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
This would include affordable housing, retail space for small traders and adequate parking.
"We should build upwards to include sufficient parking, shops for small traders, and most importantly, affordable housing for the people.
"This reduces travel costs and makes public transport even more accessible," he said at the launch of the Shah Alam LRT3 line at the Setia Johan station depot today.
Anwar said he had instructed the secretaries-general of the Transport and Finance ministries to expedite the development of such projects so that Prasarana Malaysia Bhd could remain focused on transport system efficiency.
He said that he wanted proposals for housing development on Prasarana land in Shah Alam to be processed efficiently so residents could benefit soon.
"For this Prasarana land project, I want to see a proposal processed efficiently so that people in Shah Alam can get housing soon," he said.
Anwar also touched on the need for good governance, adding that there should be no corruption or use of delays to seek commissions in project execution.
"I want to stress: do not steal money, do not engage in corruption, and do not use delays as an excuse to take commissions," he said.
Transit-oriented development
involves building high-density, mixed-use projects near transit stations.
Prasarana has used this model through its subsidiary Prasarana Integrated Development Sdn Bhd (PRIDE) for several existing rail-linked sites in the Klang Valley, including Ara Damansara, Kelana Jaya and Glenmarie.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Where are they? Dogs disappear before South Korea meat ban
Article:
ELECTRIC prods rest against the wall near rusty cages containing dog skulls in an abandoned canine slaughterhouse in a South Korean town.
The deserted site in Pyeongtaek, south of Seoul, offers a glimpse into an industry rapidly disappearing as South Korea's landmark dog meat ban enters into force next year.
It also begs a question: what happened to the hundreds of thousands of dogs once bred for human consumption?
Historically regarded as a summer stamina booster – particularly among older and rural people – dog meat has steadily fallen out of favour as younger Koreans embrace dogs as pets and public attitudes shift.
In January 2024, the country passed a law banning the breeding, slaughter and sale of dogs for food. Once it takes effect next February, violators will risk prison sentences of up to three years.
As the ban looms, the industry is disappearing much faster than many expected.
Government figures show between 400,000 and 450,000 dogs were being raised for meat in 2024. Today, the agriculture ministry estimates just 20,000 remain on farms.
There are no statistics on dog meat consumption trends, but it is widely accepted to be popular among only a small minority of South Korea's 51 million people.
To help farmers transition, the government offered up to 600,000 won (about US$390) for every dog they got rid of.
But where the animals went was not tracked.
"Our role is to verify that dogs are no longer present at farms or slaughter facilities before providing compensation," a ministry inspector told AFP on condition of anonymity.
"We are not involved in what was done with the dogs," he said.
Data obtained by a lawmaker showed that by February just 623 dogs had been adopted, and just under 500 were sent to shelters.
[[nid:1474952]]
Most of the rest were likely killed, animal welfare groups and former farmers believe.
"If large numbers of rescued dogs had entered adoption programmes, animal groups like us would know about them," said Kim Young-hwan, a representative of animal rights group CARE.
"We haven't seen any adoption campaigns for dogs rescued from dog farms," he told AFP.
In over two decades, CARE says it has rescued and rehomed about 2,500 farmed dogs, most of them sent overseas.
Only about two dozen were adopted domestically, partly due to South Koreans' preference for small breeds better suited to apartment living.
Dogs raised for eating were often large breeds like Nureongi, also known as the Korean Yellow Spitz.
"In South Korea, a distinction has long been made between dogs raised for food and those kept as pets," Ju Yeong-bong, a former dog farmer, told AFP.
Asked about the fate of the thousands of unaccounted-for canines, he acknowledged they had likely "already been eaten."
That possibility is "infuriating," said CARE's Kim. But the reality is that the country's animal welfare groups lack the resources to rescue more dogs, he added.
According to the agriculture ministry, 1,265 dog farms – about 82 per cent of the total – had applied to shut down as of May.
[[nid:1474951]]
Ju, who also serves as a Christian pastor, started his dog farm in 1994 after finding he could not make a living from his ministry alone.
"I feel the dog meat ban is a betrayal," he told AFP.
"It was imposed for political reasons, without meaningful dialogue or adequate measures to protect our livelihoods."
Many other former dog farmers are trying to switch to other livestock businesses, but lengthy government licensing has made the transition difficult, Ju added.
For activists, the change in law will not only end the consumption of dogs, but also a "long-standing loophole" around their treatment, said Park Joo-yeon, a lawyer and head of animal rights group PNR.
Unlike cattle or pigs, dogs were never legally classified as livestock in South Korea.
This meant the industry operated for decades with no rules on humane husbandry and slaughter.
Dogs were commonly electrocuted, hanged or beaten to death, animal rights activists said.
At the Pyeongtaek facility, AFP journalists saw abandoned tools allegedly used to electrocute dogs.
"They often remained conscious while their internal organs burned," Shin Joo-woon, a KARA campaigner, told AFP.
"Other dogs would witness the process."
KARA rescued 29 dogs from the Pyeongtaek farm last month and have filed a complaint against its owner for animal cruelty, which is outlawed.
* The writer is from AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Penang, Johor lead export surge as Malaysia's trade hits RM327.6b in May
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Penang and Johor spearheaded Malaysia's export growth in May, helping lift total trade to RM327.6 billion.
This comprised exports of RM184.0 billion and imports of RM143.6 billion, according to the Statistics Department.
Chief statistician Datuk Seri Dr Mohd Uzir Mahidin said exports jumped RM57.4 billion, or 45.3 per cent year-on-year, driven by stronger external demand across key manufacturing and trading hubs.
Penang was the standout performer, recording a RM50.0 billion increase in exports, followed by Johor (RM6.6 billion), Kuala Lumpur (RM2.1 billion), Sarawak (RM1.7 billion), Perak (RM504.4 million), Terengganu (RM346.0 million) and Kelantan (RM35.7 million).
However, exports declined in several states including Sabah (RM1.1 billion), Pahang (RM905.8 million), Melaka (RM569.7 million), Selangor (RM509.1 million), Labuan (RM282.9 million), Kedah (RM183.4 million), Negri Sembilan (RM154.1 million) and Perlis (RM10.3 million).
Penang remained the country's largest exporter, accounting for 52.1 per cent of total exports, followed by Johor (17.0 per cent), Selangor (12.7 per cent), Sarawak (5.0 per cent) and Kuala Lumpur (3.7 per cent).
On the import front, Malaysia's imports rose RM17.8 billion, or 14.1 per cent year-on-year, led by Penang with an increase of RM13.2 billion and Johor (RM9.2 billion).
Other states registering import growth were Kedah (RM1.3 billion), Negri Sembilan (RM1.0 billion), Kuala Lumpur (RM496.4 million), Sabah (RM245.0 million), Pahang (RM68.1 million), Labuan (RM31.5 million) and Terengganu (RM2.2 million).
Penang also dominated imports with a 29.4 per cent share, followed by Johor (24.4 per cent), Selangor (22.4 per cent), Kuala Lumpur (8.3 per cent) and Kedah (4.8 per cent).
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fadillah: 17 additional Sarawak state seats unlikely before next election
Article:
KUCHING: The proposed addition of 17 seats to the Sarawak state assembly is unlikely to be tabled in Parliament before the next state election, says Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof.
Fadillah said the Election Commission (EC) informed him that several processes related to the redelineation exercise must be completed before the matter could be brought to the Dewan Rakyat.
These processes include the public display of the proposed redelineation and a period for objections and public feedback.
"Once the proposal is displayed, there will be a period for the public to submit objections and so on. The process and procedures alone may not be completed in time for it to be tabled this year," he said after an event at Sekolah Menengah Sains Kuching Utara.
Fadillah said the proposal is expected to be tabled in Parliament next year. In the meantime, the EC will continue to engage members of parliament and senators to discuss electoral redelineation and representation.
"The purpose is to ensure MPs understand Sarawak's position and to obtain views and ideas that can be considered before the motion is tabled.
"This is so that when we debate the matter in Parliament, we know what needs to be brought forward, particularly issues required by the Sarawak government that need parliamentary support.
"We leave the matter to the EC because it has procedures that must be followed," he said.
On July 7 last year, the Sarawak State Legislative Assembly unanimously passed a bill to increase the number of state seats from 82 to 99.
Earlier, Fadillah presented 150 high-achieving Sijil Pelajaran Malaysia scorers from the school with RM500 each.
In his speech at the event, Fadillah urged students pursuing higher education to choose fields of study that align with the future development needs of Sarawak and the nation.
"We need more engineers, especially those capable of carrying out research and development, who not only possess knowledge but are also able to develop new products and materials that can offer solutions to current challenges."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Serbian President Vucic says he will resign within weeks amid student protests
Article:
BELGRADE: Serbian President Aleksandar Vucic said on Saturday he would resign within weeks and the country will hold early presidential and parliamentary elections, following 18 months of anti-government protests.
The announcement by Vucic, who has been in power as president or prime minister for 12 years, came amid persistent anti-corruption demonstrations led by students and triggered by the collapse of an awning at a railway station in the northern city of Novi Sad in November 2024, in which 16 people died.
Protesters, opposition and rights groups allege the railway station disaster was a sign of broader government mismanagement of construction projects and corruption.
"I will be president for only a couple more weeks, and then I will resign," Vucic told throngs of his supporters at a pro-government rally in the capital, Belgrade. Vucic's second and final mandate was due to expire in mid-2027.
Vucic said he would help his Serbian Progressive Party (SNS) win the presidential election and the early parliamentary vote, also originally set for 2027.
"My proposal is for our list, the winning list in the upcoming elections, to be named 'United Serbia'."
He did not specify when he would resign nor when he would dissolve parliament, a precondition for early parliamentary elections.
Vucic is unlikely to depart from the political stage as his resignation could pave the way for him to become prime minister if his party triumphs in parliamentary elections.
That would continue a long trend in which the power in Serbia follows Vucic, regardless of his title.
Analysts said Vucic would try to place an ally as his successor in the presidency so he can continue to hold the levers of power.
"This is not at all the end of Vucic," said Radivoje Grujic, a Warsaw-based analyst. "He already has a plan, one that definitely does not mean he's going to go into political retirement — quite the opposite."
Still, the timing of Saturday's announcement suggests the protests — the biggest string of rallies since the overthrow of Slobodan Milosevic in 2000 — have played a role.
Days ago, in the city of Novi Sad, students commemorated victims of the disaster and demanded snap general elections. Another student rally is scheduled for Sunday in the town of Kraljevo, in south-central Serbia.
Activists from the student-led movement and the opposition have both said they want to challenge Vucic and the SNS in the elections.
Savo Manojlovic, head of the student opposition Move-Change movement, said:
"By resigning and with early presidential and parliamentary elections, Vucic is trying to preempt his inevitable fall, because of protests and because of the student movement, which has more support than he does."
The position of president in Serbia is largely ceremonial, but Vucic has wielded considerable influence over his party and the government.
He has already floated the idea of becoming prime minister again, and recently several top allies publicly said he should.
In an interview with Reuters in February, Vucic said he was unsure what he would do after his term expires, but did not rule out returning to party politics or seeking to become prime minister.
"I would like to be less engaged in politics or not at all, but taking care of my legacy might require some sort of engagement; we'll see," he said.
During the rally in sweltering heat, Vucic told his backers, many bused in from across the country, that students and anti-government protesters were aiming to destroy the country, and accused them of colluding with unnamed foreign powers, charges the protesters deny.
"For the thousand and first time, we are offering you our hand. We forgive everything you have done, but we are not naive, and we will not forget what has been done to the country in the last year."
Serbia is a candidate to join the EU, but Belgrade still has strong ties with Russia and China — a line Vucic has had to walk throughout his time in power.
Before joining the EU, Serbia must improve its rule of law, including conditions for free and fair elections, and root out corruption and organised crime.
It also has to align its foreign policies with those of the bloc. Vucic on Saturday pledged his party would end corruption.
He promised an increase in pensions and financial allocations for the poor, and improvements in state-run health services.
But opposition figures accused Vucic and his allies of violence against political opponents, rampant corruption, ties with organised crime and stifling media freedoms.
Vucic and his allies deny these allegations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Khaled: BN's mix of new and old candidates the best formula
Article:
KLUANG: Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin has dismissed claims that Barisan Nasional (BN) is fielding "recycled" candidates for the Johor election, saying the line-up reflects a balanced mix of experienced and new faces.
He said the BN candidate list combines seasoned leaders and fresh candidates, describing it as the best formula to govern the state effectively.
Candidate selection, he said, should not be judged on whether an individual has previously contested or held office, but on capability and track record.
"If we field only new candidates, it would be like conducting an experiment.
"We cannot afford to take such a risk. Therefore, the best approach is to have a combination and blend of new and old candidates," he said when met at the Southern Zone Malaysian Army Family Day carnival here today.
He was responding to criticism by former Umno supreme council member Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi, who claimed Umno was fielding too many "recycled" candidates for the Johor polls, arguing it did not reflect party renewal or meet youth expectations.
Puad also said Umno was fielding numerous
"recycled" candidates
who failed to meet the aspirations of young voters in the current Johor election.
BN's goal, Khaled said, is not merely to showcase new faces, but to ensure candidates are capable of governing and continuing the state's development agenda if given a mandate by voters.
"We are not looking to showcase only new candidates. If they win and do not know where to start, that will also pose a problem.
"It's not as if all veteran candidates are non-performers. Some continue to work well and contribute. What matters is capability and service track record, not whether a person is old or new," he said.
He added that the increasingly complex political environment requires a balance between experience and fresh ideas so the incoming government can function effectively without a long adjustment perio
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mindef looks to four countries for new anti-ship missiles after Norway export ban
Article:
KLUANG: Malaysia has identified four potential countries as suppliers of anti-ship missile systems to replace the Norwegian naval strike missiles (NSM) after Norway cancelled its export licence to the country.
Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the ministry is evaluating offers from the four countries based on key criteria to ensure the chosen system meets the operational requirements of the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN), particularly for the Littoral Combat Ship (LCS).
He said the four countries under consideration include several European nations, as well as Turkiye and South Korea, but declined to disclose anything further as the evaluation process is ongoing.
"We have four countries we are considering. The consideration is not basic or general; we must evaluate whether the replacement missile can be integrated with the Combat Management System (CMS) on the LCS.
"We know the CMS on the LCS is French-made. So now, many countries are submitting proposals to sell replacement missiles to us," he said after attending the Karnival Hari Keluarga Kita with the Southern Zone Army, here today.
Besides compatibility with the ship's combat system, the ministry is also assessing delivery timelines as a crucial factor in accelerating the readiness of RMN assets.
He said past experience showed that the NSM order took up to eight years to be supplied, and the government now wants to choose a supplier capable of delivering the system sooner.
"If they can supply it, but it takes eight years, that is not a strength. We want to see who can deliver within one, two, or three years," he said.
He added that cost is also being factored in to ensure the price offered is reasonable, alongside missile technology that is comparable to or better than the NSM.
Among the features being evaluated are the missile's ability to evade radar detection, use flight paths that are difficult to intercept, and accurately identify targets based on ship silhouettes.
That is the kind of technology we are looking for. That is why we need to conduct a careful evaluation, he said.
The Norwegian government cancelled the
export licence
for the NSM anti-ship missiles and related launcher systems to Malaysia on the grounds of protecting its national security.
According to the Kongsberg Defence and Aerospace AS (KDA) website, the NSM procurement was inked between the RMN and KDA in April 2018, with a contract value of €124 million (RM571.9 million) to equip six new Littoral Combat Ships (LCS).
Following the cancellation, Malaysia sent a letter of demand
seeking damages
exceeding RM1 billion to the relevant company and is currently awaiting a response from the Norwegian government.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Google limits Meta's use of its Gemini AI models
Article:
LONDON: Google has put limits on Meta's use of its Gemini AI models after the social media company sought more computing capacity than the rival tech group could provide, the Financial Times reported on Sunday.
Google, owned by Alphabet, told Meta around March it could not meet the full Gemini capacity the company had sought to purchase, the newspaper said, adding that the shortfall disrupted and delayed some of Meta's internal AI projects.
Several other Google clients have also been affected, though to a lesser extent, according to the report. Meta has been particularly impacted due to its exceptionally high demand for Google's models, the FT said.
Reuters could not immediately verify the report, which cited people familiar with the matter. Google and Meta did not immediately respond to requests for comment outside business hours.
Due to the restrictions, Meta has encouraged staff to be more efficient with AI tokens, the units that measure AI usage, the FT report said.
Even as companies continue to spend billions on chips and data centres, they are still struggling to secure enough computing power to support the growing demand for AI services.
Revenue at Google Cloud grew to US$20 billion in the first quarter ended March, but CEO Sundar Pichai said computing power constraints prevented even higher growth and contributed to the cloud unit's backlog nearly doubling quarter on quarter.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Three Pakistani troops killed in terror attack in Karachi
Article:
KARACHI: Three Pakistani Rangers were killed in a terror attack in the southern megacity Karachi on Saturday evening, the country's military said, following an explosion and heavy gunfire.
A statement released Sunday by the Pakistan Armed Forces said terror group Jamaat-ul-Ahrar was responsible for the "cowardly" raid on a Rangers camp in the port city.
"The assailants, after a blast at main gate of the camp, attempted to breach the perimeter security, however, their nefarious designs were decisively foiled by the vigilant and resolute response of Rangers troops," which left three attackers dead and another wounded.
The statement added that three Rangers died and four more were wounded.
Jamaat-ul-Ahrar is a militant group often linked to Tehreek-e-Taliban Pakistan (TTP).
Attacks, many claimed by the TTP and its affiliates, have escalated in recent years in Pakistan.
Islamabad has accused Afghanistan of providing a safe haven to militants since the Afghan Taliban returned to governing the country in 2021, which Kabul has repeatedly denied.
The armed forces statement said Pakistan will "continue at full pace to wipe out menace of foreign sponsored and supported terrorism from the country", and vowed retaliation.
AFP journalists heard heavy gunfire in Karachi on Saturday night, as armed service personnel cordoned off streets around the Mosamiat Chowrangi area of the city, the capital of Sindh province.
Paramilitary forces were seen wrapped in ammunition as others moved through the streets in the back of pick-up trucks.
The Sindh chief minister Syed Murad Ali Shah said he had called for a detailed report into the incident.
Pakistan's President Asif Ali Zardari said on X he express his "heartfelt sympathy and condolences to the families of the martyrs, and prays for the speedy recovery of the injured personnel."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Onn Hafiz: Debates may fuel emotions during election campaign
Article:
SIMPANG RENGGAM: Public debates are not the most suitable approach during an election campaign, as Johor's political culture has long been centred on harmony, decorum and mutual respect.
Johor Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman and caretaker menteri besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi said debates could sometimes trigger emotional reactions among supporters, which may not be conducive to a healthy election environment.
"In Johor, we do not practise politics by engaging in verbal spats or public debates. If we look at the state assembly sittings, they have always been conducted in a harmonious and orderly manner while maintaining proper decorum," he said.
"Sometimes, when public debates are held, they invite teams and supporters who become more emotional than the individuals involved in the debate themselves.
"So, for me, there is no issue, but in the context of this election, I do not think it is something appropriate," he told reporters at the Simpang Renggam Prisons Family Day.
Also present were Deputy Home Minister and Umno Supreme Council member Datuk Seri Shamsul Anuar Nasarah, and BN candidate for the Layang-Layang seat Chua Jian Boon.
Onn Hafiz was commenting on an invitation by Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Puteri Wangsa seat, Dr Maszlee Malik, to engage in a public debate.
Earlier, Maszlee said he was prepared to debate any party, particularly Onn Hafiz, to present PH's vision and champion the interests of the people.
"I am prepared to debate anyone, especially the menteri besar, because we want to present Pakatan Harapan's vision.
"We want to bring forward a vision that represents the voices of the people, from the smallest segment of society to the largest, because that is Pakatan Harapan's raison d'être," he said after the nomination process at Sekolah Menengah Kebangsaan Bandar Dato' Onn on Saturday.
Maszlee also pledged to pursue balanced development across Johor if given the mandate in the state election.
He said a PH victory in Puteri Wangsa and across Johor was crucial to usher in a new development agenda that would not be concentrated solely in southern Johor.
Johor goes to the polls on July 11, with early voting scheduled for July 7.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: South Korea, Japan reaffirm denuclearisation goal, closer defence ties
Article:
SEOUL: South Korea and Japan on Sunday reaffirmed their commitment to the denuclearisation of the Korean peninsula and agreed to revive joint search-and-rescue drills in a step forward for security ties between the neighbouring countries.
Meeting in Seoul, South Korean Defence Minister Ahn Gyu-back and his Japanese counterpart Shinjiro Koizumi agreed to work on regional stability bilaterally, as well as through their partnerships with Washington, in the sixth round of talks between the two countries.
"Both ministers shared the view to continue cooperation for maintaining regional peace and stability amid a grave security environment," South Korea's defence ministry said in a statement.
South Korea and Japan, with U.S. encouragement, have been working to develop closer ties since 2022 and overcome sometimes bitter historical differences, a policy continued by President Lee Jae Myung and Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi.
In 2019, Seoul moved to end the GSOMIA intelligence-sharing pact with Japan after Tokyo restricted exports of semiconductor materials and removed South Korea from its preferential trade list, over lingering grievances rooted in Japan's past colonial rule of the Korean peninsula.
In 2025, Japan's then-Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba and President Lee agreed to closer security and economic ties, and the defence ministers committed to working with Washington against North Korea's nuclear threat and Pyongyang's growing military ties with Russia, including cooperation on AI and unmanned systems and annual trilateral drills.
Takaichi and Lee agreed in January 2026 to deepen shuttle diplomacy and in May expanded cooperation on energy.
On Sunday, Ahn and Koizumi also agreed to continue fostering exchange between their air forces' respective aerobatic teams — South Korea's Black Eagles and Japan's Blue Impulse — to further advance search-and-rescue exercises designed for various maritime accident scenarios.
The two previously held talks in Japan in January and met again in May at the Shangri-La Dialogue in Singapore where they discussed a possible military-logistics support agreement covering fuel, food and ammunition.
The two sides also agreed to hold a joint humanitarian search-and-rescue exercise in June, the first in almost a decade.
Tensions, however, remain, including lingering disputes over Korean women forced to work in Japanese military brothels during World War Two.
In February, Seoul protested against a Japanese government event commemorating a cluster of disputed islands known as Takeshima in Japan and Dokdo in South Korea, which controls the territory.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: All aboard: Shah Alam LRT Line opens to public tomorrow
Article:
SHAH ALAM: The
Shah Alam LRT Line
will open to the public tomorrow, supported by a network of feeder buses and Rapid KL On-Demand van services, says Transport Minister Anthony Loke.
"This integration is crucial in completing the first-mile, last-mile ecosystem, enabling users to enjoy a more integrated, convenient and efficient journey from the starting point to the final destination," he said at the launch of the line at the Johan Setia Station Depot.
Loke said the opening of the line marked a significant step in expanding a modern, integrated and world-class public transport network in the Klang Valley.
The project, involving an investment of RM16.63 billion, is among the government's strategic investments in strengthening public transport infrastructure.
"The opening of the Shah Alam Line completes another modern rail transport network connecting residential areas, commercial centres, educational institutions, hospitals and industrial areas in the western Klang Valley.
"This route not only improves connectivity, but also serves as a catalyst for economic development, more sustainable urban development, and increased public access to job opportunities and services," he said.
Loke added that the facility would offer a comfortable and reliable mobility option.
"This facility will not only save daily expenses, but also increase the connectivity and daily productivity of the people," he said.
He thanked Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim for officiating the launch ceremony, calling it a meaningful moment for the Transport Ministry, Prasarana, and all public transport users in the Klang Valley.
He also commended the Land Public Transport Agency (APAD), local authorities, and strategic partners for ensuring the new line could operate smoothly and safely.
"I hope the opening of the Shah Alam Line will be a catalyst for increased use of public transport in this country.
"More importantly, I hope this investment will continue to provide great benefits to the people, stimulate economic growth and support more sustainable urban development for the prosperity of the country," he said.
The Shah Alam Line, also known as LRT3, spans 37.8km with 20 stations connecting Bandar Utama in Petaling Jaya to Johan Setia in Klang.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MMC rolls out Semak Berita to tackle AI-driven misinformation in Johor polls
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Media Council (MMC) has launched Semak Berita, a public initiative to help verify dubious, fabricated or misused election-related information during the Johor  election period.S
Chairperson of the council, Tan Sri Nallini Pathmanathan, said the initiative addresses growing concerns over the misuse of media identities during elections.
During election periods, false information no longer appears merely as rumours, MMC said in a statement.
It can take the form of convincing news graphics, fabricated screenshots, misappropriated media logos, invented statements, illegitimate election results, or content generated and manipulated using artificial intelligence (AI) technologies.
MMC said when the names and logos of legitimate media organisations are used to spread falsehoods, the damage extends beyond a single newsroom.
Public trust in the entire media ecosystem is undermined, MMC said.
"The Malaysian Media Council is not an arbiter of political truth. We do not adjudicate manifestos, campaign claims, or debates between political parties," Nallini said.
She said Semak Berita is focused on a clear verification function.
"The line we draw is straightforward: was this content genuinely published by the newsroom in question? That is a factual matter that can be verified quickly," she said.
The Semak Berita pilot mechanism will operate throughout the election period and act as a coordination platform for rapid verification and information sharing among stakeholders.
Participating media organisations will appoint primary and alternate verification officers to respond during the election period.
When content is disputed — including fake graphics using media logos, manipulated screenshots or false claims on polling centres, voting procedures or election results — the MMC will coordinate verification with relevant parties.
For election-related procedural matters such as polling centres, voting processes and official results, issues will be referred to the Election Commission as the relevant authority.
Where content involves specific media organisations, verification will be carried out directly with those organisations.
"When the names and logos of legitimate media organisations are used to spread falsehoods, the damage extends beyond a single newsroom," Nallini said.
The initiative will run as a pilot throughout the election period to support rapid verification and strengthen public confidence in information circulating online.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: BN’s mix of experience, fresh faces key to governing state, says Khaled Nordin
Article:
KLUANG, June 28 — Barisan Nasional’s (BN) blend of experienced leaders and fresh faces for the 16th Johor state election is the best formula for meeting the increasingly complex challenges of governing, Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said.
He said criticism that BN was relying heavily on ‘recycled’ candidates should be viewed in the context of the need to ensure continuity in leadership and experience within the state administration.
According to Mohamed Khaled, fielding an entirely new line-up would amount to an unnecessary gamble at a time when experienced leaders are needed to navigate increasingly complex challenges.
“We must remember that the situation we face today is very complex. If we field only new candidates, it would be like experimenting. We simply cannot afford that.
“That is why the best approach is to have a combination of experienced and new candidates,” he told reporters after attending the Southern Zone Armed Forces Family Day Carnival at X Park Impiana 2 Square, Bandar Seri Impian, here today.
The Defence Minister and Kota Tinggi MP said experience is crucial to ensuring a government can function effectively once entrusted with the people’s mandate.
He said BN was not fielding new faces merely to satisfy public perception, but was instead prioritising candidates capable of delivering on their responsibilities if elected.
“We are not trying to showcase an entirely new line-up only for them to be unsure where to begin after winning. What matters is the ability to work and serve the people,” he said.
He added that Johoreans need a strong and stable government to address current challenges and continue the state’s development agenda, stressing that elections should enable voters to choose a government capable of delivering a better future.
Mohamed Khaled also argued that only BN and Pakatan Harapan (PH) are in a position to form the next Johor state government based on the number of seats they are contesting.
Following nominations yesterday, BN and PH are each contesting all 56 seats, while Perikatan Nasional (PN), through PAS and Bersatu, is contesting 27 seats.
“If you look at the nominations, only BN and PH have the numbers to form the state government. PAS is contesting 11 seats and Bersatu 16. Even combined, they cannot form the state government,” he said.
Meanwhile, in PONTIAN, Johor BN deputy chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Maslan said the selection of all 56 candidates was based on a thorough assessment, taking into account a balanced mix of new faces, experienced leaders, women and youth candidates.
The Umno supreme council member said BN had also fielded technocrats and professionals from various backgrounds, including lawyers, doctors and engineers.
“We are highly confident because the candidates presented to the people have been assessed from every angle,” he told a press conference after officiating the Meh Lah Pontian Community Programme: Kelana Keringat in conjunction with the National International Museum Day Festival 2026.
Polling is on July 11, with early voting on July 7.
For the latest updates on the 16th Johor state election, visit https://prn.bernama.com/johor/. — Bernama

Title: DPM Fadillah: Proposal for 17 new Sarawak seats unlikely this year as EC procedures ongoing
Article:
KUCHING, June 28 — The proposal to add 17 new seats to the Sarawak Legislative Assembly is unlikely to be tabled in the Dewan Rakyat this year, said Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Fadillah Yusof.
He said the matter must go through several processes and procedures under the Election Commission (EC), including public display exercises to allow members of the public to submit objections or feedback.
“Based on information from the EC, it is most likely that it will not be tabled this year as several processes and procedures still need to be carried out.
“At the earliest, the EC can first display it to the public. After the display, there will be a period for objections and other processes. So it is most likely that it can only be tabled next year,” he told reporters after handing over incentives to students who achieved excellent results in their SPM examination at Sekolah Menengah Sains Kuching Utara here today.
Fadillah, who is also Parti Pesaka Bumiputera Bersatu (PBB) senior vice-president, said the EC has its own procedures involving studies and population census data.
“The determination of when the proposal will be tabled is entirely under the jurisdiction of the EC, without government interference,” he said.
In July last year, the State Legislative Assembly passed a Bill to add 17 new state seats, increasing the total from 82 to 99, subject to the redelineation process by the EC. — Bernama

Title: Onn Hafiz says open debates not Johor’s way after offer from Maszlee Malik
Article:
SIMPANG RENGGAM, June 28 — Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has reportedly said open debates between candidates may not be suitable for the Johor state election, adding that they could disrupt the harmony of the campaign period.
According to
Buletin TV3
, he said Johor’s political culture has long placed importance on decorum, restraint and orderly debate, rather than open exchanges that could turn confrontational.
“I see that our way in Johor is not to argue or debate openly. If we look at the state assembly sitting, it is not like that. It is harmonious, orderly and observes decorum,” he was quoted as saying at the Prison Family Day programme in Kampung Paya Mas today.
He added that open debates could sometimes draw supporters who were more emotional than the candidates themselves, making them unsuitable in the context of an election campaign.
This comes after Pakatan Harapan’s candidate for the Puteri Wangsa seat, Datuk Maszlee Malik, had expressed his willingness to debate any party, particularly Onn Hafiz, during the Johor campaign period.

Title: Malaysia shortlists four countries for new LCS anti-ship missile system, says defence minister
Article:
KLUANG, June 28 — The Ministry of Defence (Mindef) has shortlisted four countries as possible suppliers of an anti-ship missile system for the Littoral Combat Ship (LCS) project after Norway cancelled the Naval Strike Missile (NSM) procurement.
Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said the selection process will focus on key criteria to ensure the chosen system meets the operational needs of the Royal Malaysian Navy (RMN) beyond basic specifications.
He said the countries being considered are Türkiye, South Korea and two European countries.
“We must evaluate whether the replacement missile can be integrated with the LCS’s French-built Combat Management System (CMS). It must be fully interoperable with the existing system,” he told reporters at the Southern Zone Malaysian Armed Forces (MAF) Karnival Hari Keluarga Kita at X Park Impiana 2 Square, Bandar Seri Impian, here today.
He was commenting on the latest developments following the Norwegian government’s revocation of the export licence for the NSM to Malaysia.
Mohamed Khaled said the government is reviewing the delivery timeline to avoid delays that could affect the country’s maritime defence capabilities.
“We want to know who can supply the missiles earliest. Some are still developing their systems, but if the delivery timeline takes up to eight years, that is not a viable option for us,” he said.
He added that pricing is also a key factor to ensure the government obtains a high-technology system at a reasonable cost.
He said the selected missile must have capabilities similar to the NSM, such as stealth, sea-skimming to evade enemy air defence, and high-precision targeting.
The Norwegian government had cancelled the NSM export licence to Malaysia on the grounds of protecting its national security interests.
Following the cancellation, Mindef has issued an RM1 billion claim against the relevant defence firm over the aborted NSM procurement.
The LCS project is a key strategic asset for the RMN and strengthens the country’s maritime defence. — Bernama

Title: Govt mulls affordable housing on LRT3 park-and-ride sites to boost public transport use
Article:
KLANG, June 28 — The government is planning to redevelop large park-and-ride sites along the newly launched LRT3 Shah Alam Line into affordable housing and commercial projects, in a move aimed at making better use of public land while encouraging greater use of public transport.
Transport Minister Anthony Loke said the proposal would focus on selected stations with sizable parking facilities, rather than every station along the line, with Sri Andalas station serving as the initial example.
He said Prasarana had identified large parcels of land around some stations that are currently used solely as surface car parks, despite their development potential.
“For example, the Prime Minister boarded the train today from Sri Andalas station. We have a parking facility there that accommodates 500 vehicles. It is a large piece of land.
“If we simply leave it as a car park, it would be an inefficient use of the land. The land has value and can be developed. This is not only to generate returns for Prasarana, but more importantly to stimulate development in the surrounding area,” he said in his speech during the launch of the LRT3 Shah Alam Line today.
Loke said he had sought Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s mandate to allow Prasarana to speed up such developments, particularly for affordable housing near rail stations.
He said the projects would be undertaken with private developers on government-owned land belonging to Prasarana, while ensuring the homes remain affordable.
“The objective is to provide more affordable housing close to urban areas and public transport stations.
“When residents live next to a station, they can simply walk to the train instead of relying on another mode of transport. That makes public transport a practical choice for their daily commute,” he told reporters later.
Loke said the developments would retain parking facilities by incorporating them into basement levels, while the upper floors could house affordable homes and modest commercial lots.
He added that not every station would be suitable for redevelopment, as many are already surrounded by established developments.
Anwar, who endorsed the proposal in his speech, said large tracts of land surrounding rail stations should not be left underutilised.
He said future developments should prioritise public housing rather than high-end residential projects, alongside sufficient parking and affordable retail space for small businesses.
“I don’t think five-star housing should be built there. It should be public housing, together with modest retail lots that benefit ordinary people,” he said.
Anwar also instructed the Transport Ministry, the Finance Ministry and Prasarana to expedite planning for the developments through a dedicated unit, allowing Prasarana to remain focused on operating an efficient public transport system.
He said integrating housing with MRT and LRT stations would reduce commuting costs, improve accessibility and generate better commercial returns from government-owned land.
The prime minister also expressed confidence that the Selangor government and local authorities would cooperate in facilitating the proposed developments.
The LRT3 is scheduled to begin operations tomorrow, with Anwar announcing free fares for both the train and feeder bus services for one month.

Title: Police arrest four men, seize roosters and gambling items in Mukah cockfighting raid
Article:
MUKAH, June 28 — Four local men were arrested for allegedly involved in cockfighting activities during a police raid at an open area within an oil palm plantation along Jalan Bayan Ulu here yesterday.
Mukah district police chief DSP Muhamad Rizal Alias said the raid was carried out at about 2pm by officers from the Criminal Investigation Division of the Mukah District Police Headquarters (IPD Mukah), following intelligence and public information.
“During the raid, a group of individuals was found engaging in cockfighting activities. Upon noticing the presence of police, all of them fled into a nearby forest area,” he said in a statement.
He added that police then pursued the suspects and managed to detain four local men, aged between 30 and 48.
“Police also seized three live roosters, one dead chicken, two cockfighting spurs, a weighing scale, various gambling paraphernalia, and RM55 in cash,” he said.
All suspects were taken to IPD Mukah for further investigation.
Urine screening tests on all four suspects returned negative for drugs. However, checks revealed that one of them had a prior criminal record.
The case is being investigated under Section 73(1)(g) of the Veterinary Public Health Ordinance 1999, which provides for a fine not exceeding RM2,000, imprisonment of up to six months, or both upon conviction.
Muhamad Rizal reminded the public not to engage in illegal gambling activities, warning that such offences not only lead to arrest and prosecution but may also cause financial hardship and social problems. — The Borneo Post

Title: Father’s role important in shielding children from social ills as Madani govt bolsters family‑focused policies, says DPM Fadillah
Article:
CYBERJAYA, June 28 — A strong and effective fatherly role can serve as the earliest layer of protection in preventing children from being drawn into social ills, drug abuse and the erosion of values.
